chapter_title.ar: باب الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي يُوجِبُ التَّيَمُّمَ وَ مَنْ تَيَمَّمَ ثُمَّ وَجَدَ الْمَاءَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 41 – The timing which Obligates the Tayammum, and the one who performs Tayammum, then finds the water

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/176)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/176)
text: إِذَا لَمْ يَجِدِ الْمُسَافِرُ الْمَاءَ فَلْيَطْلُبْ مَا دَامَ فِي الْوَقْتِ فَإِذَا خَافَ أَنْ يَفُوتَهُ الْوَقْتُ فَلْيَتَيَمَّمْ وَ لْيُصَلِّ فِي آخِرِ الْوَقْتِ فَإِذَا وَجَدَ الْمَاءَ فَلَا قَضَاءَ عَلَيْهِ وَ لْيَتَوَضَّأْ لِمَا يَسْتَقْبِلُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ibn Azina, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5th or 6th Imamasws) having said: ‘When the traveller cannot find the water, so let him seek for as long as he is within the time (for the Salaat). But if he fears missing the time, so let him perform Tayammum, and let him pray Salaat during the end time. So when he does find the water, there would be no repayment (of the Salaat) upon him, and let him perform Ablution (Wudhu) for what is next (Salaat)’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from ibn ’Udhaynah from Zurarah who has said the following: “One of the two Imam, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, has said, ‘If during a journey water cannot be found, one must search for water as long as there is time; but when there is fear for losing time, one must then take Tayammum to perform Salah (prayer) during the end of time just before it totally ends. If later water is found, then one is not required to perform Salah (prayer) again; however, Wudu’ is necessary for future.’”
